steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The word “Unlike” sets up a contrast: Saeki Yūzō adopted European methods, so Hashimoto must have done the opposite — followed or stayed with traditional Japanese approaches. The following sentence confirms it: Hashimoto used ink and color on silk, a classical Japanese technique.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — refrained from: Means “held back from.” If Hashimoto refrained from traditional Japanese approaches, he would have avoided them — the opposite of what the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice B — awarded: Means “gave a prize to.” A person cannot award a technique or approach; the word is semantically incompatible with the object “traditional Japanese approaches.” ✗
  • Choice C — adhered to: Means “followed closely” or “stayed true to.” Hashimoto used ink and light color on silk — traditional Japanese methods — confirming he adhered to them. ✓
  • Choice D — reprimanded: Means “scolded.” A person cannot reprimand a set of artistic approaches; the word is semantically incompatible with the object. ✗
That is the correct answer.
